observations.description: The market experiences a significant and rapid price decline over a short period, indicating panic selling.
Trading volume is significantly higher than the average, suggesting heightened trading activity and potential panic selling.
The daily price range (high-low) is unusually wide, reflecting increased volatility and market instability.
A noticeable decrease in daily trading volume after a period of high volume, indicating a potential reduction in selling pressure and market stabilization.
A recent session has experienced a significant increase in volume, indicating a panic sell-off event.
There is a noticeable sharp decline in closing prices over the recent sessions, reflecting the panic sell-off.
Following the sharp decline, trading sessions show a reduced intraday range, indicating a decrease in volatility and potential exhaustion of selling pressure.
Volume is declining after the initial spike, reflecting a withdrawal of panicked sellers and less aggressive selling activity.
The market experiences a significant increase in volume, indicating heightened trading activity commonly associated with a sell-off.
There is a substantial decline in the closing price, reflecting a significant sell-off.
The daily high-low price range begins to narrow, suggesting a reduction in volatility and selling pressure.
Volume begins to decrease from previously heightened levels, indicating a reduction in trading activity and potentially less selling pressure.
The market has experienced a prolonged and significant decline in price over several days, indicating extended panic-driven selling.
There is a notable surge in trading volume, suggesting heightened activity often associated with panic selling.
A reduction in daily price range, implying a decrease in volatility which may indicate a pause in aggressive selling pressure.
An increase in closing prices from intra-day lows, suggesting early signs of buying interest as prices recover slightly by the end of the trading day.
A recent sharp decline in price over several days indicating a pronounced sell-off.
A substantial increase in trading volume during the sell-off, but not the highest volume recorded recently.
A noticeable decline in volume following the volume spike, indicating reduced selling pressure.
Price levels have moved significantly away from recent average levels, implying a temporary distortion.
The frequency of new lows is decreasing, suggesting a weakening of selling interest.
